svg.title: dynabatch increases batch size as predicted memory drops
svg.desc: Fixed batching keeps the same number of examples per step, causing shorter batches to use less of the memory budget. dynabatch keeps the first hardest batch as the budget and increases later batch sizes while predicted memory stays under that budget.
svg.text: dynabatch: grow the batch when the sequence gets easier
Sort by length, use the first hardest batch as the memory budget, then fit more shorter examples later.
safe memory budget from first batch
Batch size stays N=2, so memory use falls as examples get shorter.
Batch size grows N=2 → 3 → 4. Predicted memory stays near budget.
